input.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

raw.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

winner: I knew Ed Sheeran was planning ahead—turns out he even picked his own graveyard spot for the posthumous album release party. "Thinking Out Loud" took on a whole new meaning when they found him sketching headstones with song lyrics and a list labeled 'Tombstone Hits.'

candidates.text: I guess Ed Sheeran's will is finally out.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'will' (legal document) and 'will' (desire), concise and surprising with a pop culture reference."}

candidates.text: I knew Ed Sheeran was planning ahead—turns out he even picked his own graveyard spot for the posthumous album release party. "Thinking Out Loud" took on a whole new meaning when they found him sketching headstones with song lyrics and a list labeled 'Tombstone Hits.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific, with a clever twist on Ed Sheeran's song title and the concept of a posthumous album."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran is really taking his career to the grave, but at least he's making sure it’s a chart-topping burial.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever play on 'chart-topping burial' that ties back to Ed Sheeran's music success."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album was such a hit that his pre-natal album is getting re-released.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clever play on 'pre-natal' and 'posthumous,' concise and specific."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album is already finished — he just hasn't told his fans yet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Concise, surprising, and specific with a touch of dark humor that aligns well with the headline."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album is actually quite impressive, though the tracklist is a bit repetitive since he's still technically alive to write the lyrics.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a clever play on 'posthumous' and 'still alive,' concise and specific."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran is so organized that he’s already booked a tour bus for his ghost, though he’s still waiting on the reaper to confirm the final tour dates.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on planning a posthumous album, with clever use of 'ghost' and 'reaper' for humor."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album is called "Sheeran of Us." The tracklist includes "Thinking Out Loud" and... well, that's about all he could think of before passing away.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a play on Ed Sheeran's name, and includes a mild surprise with the tracklist reference. However, it lacks deeper specificity and could be more original."}

candidates.text: He’s already written the lyrics — now he just needs to die and let someone else handle the mixing.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the dark humor around death may be off-putting to some and feels a bit generic in its approach."}
